figures.caption: Figure 4 Comparison of Frame Selection. on the office/seq-07 sequence of 7-Scenes (500 frames, 𝐾=47). (a) Top-𝐾 clusters frames around a single peak, covering only 3/8 high-relevance segments. (b) Segment Sampling (Ours) distributes frames across 8 segments above threshold 𝜏 (yellow regions), reducing Accuracy error from 0.281 m to 0.036 m (7.8×).

Table 3 Ablation Studies on 7-Scenes (500 frames). (a) Descriptor feature for frame selection. (b) Selection strategy and threshold. (c) Frame budget 𝑁 (1 anchor + 𝑁−1 selected). (d) Compression strategy (both compressed variants retain about 320 active keyframes). (e) Spatial grid resolution.

Figure 10 Qualitative Comparison of 3D reconstruction on 7Scenes. Compared with TTT3R and InfiniteVGGT, RetrieveVGGT (Ours) produces more complete and geometrically consistent reconstructions with fewer outliers.

Figure 1 Formulation of RetrieveVGGT. Given streaming video input (Bottom), RetrieveVGGT retrieves relevant keyframes from history for each incoming query frame via attention-derived relevance (Middle), ensuring flexible and comprehensive scene coverage with long history (Top).

Figure 5 Pose-Aware Spatial Memory pipeline. (a) Spatial Region Assignment: each frame is mapped into an isometric 𝐾3×𝐷 grid cell indexed by its 3D position and viewing direction, with a grow-only bounding box that keeps assignments stable across deletions. (b) Periodic Compression: regions exceeding the mean occupancy are thinned via uniform-stride sampling while anchor remains protected; deleted entries are tombstoned so metadata persists.
Figure 6 Influence of input length on ATE on TUM-dynamics (50–1000 frames). Inset: short-sequence regime (≤150 frames). OOM runs truncated at their last successful length.

Table 1 3D reconstruction results on 7-Scenes [61] and NRGBD [62] at sequence lengths of 200, 300, 400, and 500 frames. Best results are in red, second best are blue. “OOM” indicates out-of-memory failure.

Figure 2 Retrieval Mechanism of RetrieveVGGT. For each frame 𝐼𝑡 : (1) Query-Driven Frame Selection: mean pooled query is compared against all history frame descriptors at the first attention layer, requiring no extra learned module. (2) Segment Sampling: selects relevant frames across distinct segments to avoid over-concentration. KV history at the selected indices is reused across all 𝐿 layers. (3) Pose-Aware Spatial Memory: organizes KV states by estimated camera poses and periodically compresses over-populated regions to bound memory with spatial diversity.

Table 7 Video depth estimation on short sequences. We report scale-invariant relative depth (Per-sequence Scale) on Sintel [69] (50 frames), Bonn [63] (110 frames), and KITTI [70] (110 frames). Best results are in bold, second best are underlined. †Base model of RetrieveVGGT.

Table 6 Camera pose estimation on short sequences. We evaluate on Sintel [69] (50 frames) and TUM-dynamics [64] (90 frames). Best results are in bold, second best are underlined. †Base model of RetrieveVGGT.
Figure 8 Retrieved keyframes on 7-Scenes [61]. Same layout as Fig. 7. The retrieved frames consistently cover geometrically relevant views from diverse temporal locations.

Table 2 Video Depth Estimation on Bonn. We report Abs Rel↓ and 𝛿<1.25 (↑) under per-sequence scale alignment at sequence lengths of 200–500 frames. Best in red, second best blue. “OOM” indicates out-of-memory failure.
Figure 9 Qualitative 3D reconstruction on NRGBD. Point cloud reconstructions on NRGBD scenes, all visualized from an identical viewpoint.

Figure 3 Analysis of Cache and Retrieval Strategy of History Frames. (a) Cosine similarity of key descriptors across 150 frames reveals high redundancy in KV cache (mean similarity ≈ 0.98), motivating subset selection. (b) Comparison of three cache management approaches: (b1) Window-based methods lose all information outside their window; (b2) Cache pruning maintains a globally compressed cache not tailored to each query, losing relevant information; (b3) RetrieveVGGT (Ours) dynamically selects the most relevant frames from the entire history based on the current query 𝑞𝑡 .

Figure 7 Retrieved keyframes on NRGBD [62]. The retrieved keyframes capture views of the same spatial region as the query and span a wide temporal range, confirming that Segment Sampling prevents clustering around a single peak.
